adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: JLR's strategy board must formally record Agratas Somerset
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: JLR's Chief Strategy Officer must, before the FY2026 Annual Report sign-off, table a formal board resolution amending the Electrification Transition Principal Risk entry to explicitly name Agratas Somerset (Gravity Smart Campus) as a single-source cell supplier, record the confirmed SOP date of late 2027, and acknowledge that the JV legal architecture does not, as reported, include OEM customer remedies for delay — with the amended register extract signed by the CEO and Chief Risk Officer as evidence of completion. The resolution must further record that no alternative cell supplier is currently qualified at JLR vehicle-architecture volumes in any region, and that AESC interim supply (as reported) does not constitute a qualified alternate for full programme volumes. Board sign-off minutes and the marked-up risk register entry constitute the required completion evidence.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a single-source dependency to be formally registered; this adaptation forces JLR to name the specific omission — that the existing Electrification Transition risk line carries no reference to Agratas Somerset, no confirmed SOP date, and no acknowledgement of the structural absence of contractual delay remedies — closing the precise gap the incident identifies.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: JLR must commission a scenario analysis quantifying the
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: JLR's Group Finance and Strategy function must commission, and deliver to the JLR Board strategy review no later than 30 days before that review's scheduled date, a quantified scenario analysis modelling the EBITDA and free-cash-flow impact on the Range Rover Electric programme across two confirmed stress cases: (a) Agratas Somerset SOP confirmed at late 2027, and (b) Somerset SOP slipping to late 2028, the latter treated as a planning contingency given the reported National Grid Gravity substation infrastructure delay into 2028. The analysis must include Castle Bromwich and Solihull production-scheduling implications, AESC interim-supply cost differential, and revenue deferral from Range Rover Electric; it must be reviewed by the Audit Committee before the strategy session and the sign-off memo from the CFO constitutes completion evidence. If the reported one-third reduction in Range Rover Electric volume cadence versus the November 2025 product plan is confirmed during the analysis period, that revised volume baseline must be incorporated as the primary scenario rather than held as a sensitivity.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a scenario analysis to exist before a board review; this adaptation anchors the two specific stress cases to the confirmed SOP slip and the reported grid infrastructure delay, preventing the analysis from being scoped around optimistic assumptions that are already inconsistent with known facts.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: JLR must identify and initiate qualification of at
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: JLR's Chief Procurement Officer must, within 60 days of this control's adoption, initiate a formal supplier qualification programme targeting at least one cell supplier — outside the Agratas/Tata group — capable of meeting Range Rover Electric platform architecture volumes at the cell chemistry and form-factor specified for Agratas Somerset Phase 1, and must confirm in writing to JLR's General Counsel, before any qualification agreement is signed, that the proposed arrangement does not trigger the grant recovery or exclusivity conditions attached to the confirmed £380 million UK government grant to Agratas (part of the wider £700 million advanced manufacturing package announced April 2026). Completion evidence is: (a) a signed qualification initiation letter to at least one candidate supplier, (b) a written legal opinion or HM Government clarification confirming grant condition compliance, and (c) an 18-month qualification milestone plan tabled at the Supply Chain Risk Committee — all three required before the 18-month deadline.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a secondary supplier to be qualified within 18 months; this adaptation makes the £380 million grant condition check a gating prerequisite — not an afterthought — because the confirmed existence of that grant creates a structural constraint that could render a qualification programme commercially and legally void if executed without prior clearance.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: JLR's investment committee must stress-test Tata Sons' FY2026-27
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: JLR's Investment Committee must, before the next scheduled JLR Audit Committee meeting and no later than end of Q2 FY2027, commission a structured capital-allocation stress test examining whether Tata Sons' FY2026–27 group-level funding commitments — including, if the reported Air India refinancing, Tata Power CapEx cycle, and Tata Capital IPO preparation are confirmed — can be accommodated concurrently with the Agratas tranche-3 funding requirement for the £4 billion Somerset gigafactory without deferral of the confirmed late-2027 SOP date. The stress test must model at minimum two scenarios: full tranche-3 funding on schedule and a six-month tranche deferral, with quantified SOP-slip and JLR revenue consequences for each; findings must be presented in writing to the JLR Audit Committee with a named executive accountable for monitoring. The Investment Committee chair's sign-off memo and Audit Committee minutes recording receipt of the analysis constitute completion evidence.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires capital-allocation stress-testing; this adaptation names the specific competing Tata Sons demands as conditional variables to be tested, preventing the stress test from being scoped narrowly around Agratas in isolation when the confirmed £4 billion investment scale means group-level capital competition is a plausible and material mechanism for SOP deferral.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: JLR must seek to insert
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: JLR's General Counsel and Chief Procurement Officer must jointly prepare, before the next scheduled Agratas JV governance review (and no later than six months from this control's adoption), a formal written proposal to insert an OEM customer remedy clause into the Agratas JV governance framework, specifying at minimum: a binding SOP milestone notification obligation, a financial remedy schedule (structured as liquidated damages or equivalent mechanism) triggered by SOP delay beyond 90 days from the confirmed late-2027 target, and a step-in or alternative-sourcing right triggered by delay beyond 180 days — with each remedy benchmarked against protections available in arm's-length Tier-1 cell supply agreements. If the reported absence of OEM customer remedy provisions in the current JV legal architecture is confirmed during preparation, that confirmation must be included in the proposal as the baseline gap being remediated. The tabled proposal and JV governance review minutes recording its formal consideration constitute completion evidence.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a customer remedy clause to be sought; this adaptation specifies the minimum remedy architecture — notification obligation, liquidated-damages schedule, step-in right — and benchmarking methodology, preventing the proposal from being satisfied by a nominal acknowledgement clause that does not constitute a commercially equivalent protection.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: JLR's third-party risk function must classify Agratas as
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: JLR's Chief Risk Officer must, within 45 days of this control's adoption, formally reclassify Agratas in JLR's third-party risk register from its current category to a standalone Tier-0 Concentration Risk designation — distinct from the standard related-party or Tier-1 supplier classification — with an enhanced monitoring protocol that includes: quarterly review of Tata Sons group-level capital-allocation announcements for evidence of Agratas tranche-3 funding deferral, real-time flagging of any public Tata group capital markets activity (bond issuance, IPO filing, rated-debt restructuring) that could signal competing funding demands, and a defined escalation threshold that triggers immediate Audit Committee notification. The reclassification must be formally ratified by the Chief Risk Officer and Supply Chain Risk Committee chair, and the updated register entry with the new designation and monitoring protocol constitutes completion evidence.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires enhanced third-party monitoring; this adaptation creates a structurally distinct risk tier for Agratas that captures the specific mechanism by which the concentration risk materialises — Tata Sons group capital-allocation decisions, not Agratas operational performance alone — requiring monitoring at the parent-group level rather than the supplier level.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: JLR must require Agratas to provide quarterly SOP-milestone attestations
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: JLR's Supply Chain Risk Committee must, effective from the quarter following this control's adoption, require Agratas to submit a written quarterly SOP-milestone attestation covering at minimum three workstreams: (a) National Grid Gravity Smart Campus substation energisation status and revised commissioning date, given the reported grid infrastructure delay into 2028; (b) Phase 1 building and process-equipment installation completion percentage against the confirmed late-2027 SOP schedule; and (c) cell qualification status against JLR Range Rover Electric platform specification. Each attestation must be signed by an Agratas officer of at least Director level, tabled as a standing agenda item at the JLR Supply Chain Risk Committee, and any attestation showing red status on any workstream must trigger an automatic escalation to the JLR Chief Risk Officer within five business days. The Supply Chain Risk Committee secretary's record of first attestation receipt and tabling constitutes initial completion evidence; ongoing compliance is evidenced by quarterly committee minutes.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires quarterly attestations; this adaptation specifies the three distinct workstream components — grid infrastructure, physical build, and cell qualification — that each independently could drive SOP slip based on the confirmed and reported facts, preventing attestations from being satisfied by a single aggregate milestone that could mask individual workstream failures.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: JLR's manufacturing operations team must document a cell-supply
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: JLR's VP Manufacturing Operations must, within 90 days of this control's adoption, produce and have approved by the JLR Investment Committee a documented cell-supply bridge plan based on the reported AESC interim supply arrangement, quantifying: (a) the maximum monthly cell volume AESC can commit to JLR under current or extended terms through Q4 2028, expressed in GWh and vehicle-equivalent units for the Range Rover Electric platform; (b) the per-unit cost premium versus the Agratas Somerset contracted cost; (c) the gross margin impact on the Range Rover Electric programme at that volume and cost differential; and (d) the minimum viable launch volume threshold below which the Range Rover Electric commercial case does not clear JLR's investment hurdle rate. The plan must explicitly address the scenario in which Agratas Somerset SOP slips to late 2028 due to the reported National Grid substation delay, and must identify whether AESC capacity is sufficient to sustain that full extended bridge period. The Investment Committee approval memo and the bridge plan document constitute completion evidence.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a bridge plan to be documented; this adaptation anchors the bridge plan to the specific reported AESC arrangement and forces a direct quantification of whether AESC capacity is actually sufficient to cover the full extended slip scenario — a question the confirmed grid infrastructure delay makes concrete rather than hypothetical.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: JLR must update its Electrification Transition operational continuity
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: JLR's Chief Operating Officer must, within 60 days of this control's adoption, direct the Electrification Transition operational continuity plan to be updated to reflect the confirmed SOP slip of Agratas Somerset from late 2026 to late 2027, incorporating at minimum: revised production scheduling for Castle Bromwich (Jaguar all-electric GT) and Solihull (Range Rover Electric) that reflects actual cell availability under the confirmed late-2027 SOP date and the contingency of a further slip to late 2028; and if the reported approximately one-third reduction in Range Rover Electric volume cadence versus the November 2025 product plan is confirmed during the update process, that revised volume baseline must replace the November 2025 plan as the scheduling input. The updated plan must be reviewed and signed off by the COO and tabled at the next JLR Board Operations Committee; the tabling record and signed updated plan constitute completion evidence.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires an operational continuity plan update; this adaptation names the specific manufacturing sites and programmes affected and makes the volume cadence reduction a mandatory re-baselining input if confirmed — preventing the plan from being updated to reflect the SOP slip while retaining an optimistic volume assumption that is already reported to be inconsistent with the current programme state.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: JLR's geopolitical risk register must explicitly flag the
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: JLR's Chief Risk Officer must, before the next annual geopolitical risk register review and no later than end of Q3 FY2027, insert a dedicated entry into JLR's geopolitical risk register titled 'BEV Cell Supply Single-Site Concentration — Somerset/Tata Group' that explicitly records: (a) the confirmed Agratas Somerset Gravity Smart Campus as the sole site providing cell supply for the entire JLR BEV programme, operated by a Tata group subsidiary; (b) the confirmed absence of any currently qualified alternative cell supplier at JLR vehicle-architecture volumes in any other UK, EU, or non-UK jurisdiction; (c) the confirmed late-2027 SOP date and the reported National Grid substation infrastructure delay into 2028 as the primary near-term geopolitical and infrastructure concentration vectors; and (d) an explicit note that the AESC interim arrangement, as reported, does not constitute a geographically diversified or volume-equivalent alternative. The entry must be reviewed by the Board Risk Committee and the signed register extract constitutes completion evidence.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a geopolitical concentration flag to be entered; this adaptation forces the register entry to record the jurisdictional single-point explicitly — all qualified BEV cell supply in one Somerset site — and to distinguish the AESC interim arrangement from a genuine geographic diversification, preventing the entry from being satisfied by a disclosure that implies greater resilience than the confirmed facts support.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: JLR must assess whether the £380M UK government grant conditions
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: JLR's General Counsel must, within 45 days of this control's adoption, commission a formal legal analysis — from external counsel with UK public procurement and state-aid grant experience — of the conditions attached to the confirmed £380 million UK government grant to Agratas (announced by Business Secretary Peter Kyle on 9 April 2026, forming part of the wider £700 million advanced manufacturing package), specifically to determine whether those conditions restrict JLR's ability to qualify a non-Agratas or non-UK cell supplier for the Range Rover Electric or Jaguar GT platform without triggering grant recovery, clawback, or re-opening obligations. The legal analysis must be delivered in writing, must address the cell-chemistry flexibility question directly (i.e. whether a chemistry change to accommodate a different supplier also triggers grant conditions), and must be tabled at the JLR Audit Committee with a recommended course of action. The external counsel opinion, General Counsel cover note, and Audit Committee tabling record constitute completion evidence.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires an assessment of grant condition restrictions; this adaptation names the specific confirmed grant (£380 million, 9 April 2026, Business Secretary Peter Kyle) and requires the legal analysis to address the chemistry-flexibility question explicitly — because the reported architecture-lock concern arises not only from supplier exclusivity terms but potentially from the cell-specification conditions embedded in the grant, a distinct legal question that a generic grant-condition review could miss.
